# The Pacific War — Final Year

The brutal end of WWII in the Pacific.

**Case: Iwo Jima. 7,000 Americans died for a tiny island. The flag raising became the symbol of the war.**

**Case: The Firebombing. 334 B-29s firebombed Tokyo. 100,000 civilians died in one night.**

**Case: Hiroshima. The Enola Gay dropped Little Boy. 140,000 died. The atomic age began.**

**Case: Nagasaki. The second bomb fell on August 9. Japan surrendered six days later.**

**Case: Okinawa. The bloodiest battle. 77,000 US casualties. 150,000 civilians died.**

**Case: The Enola Gay. The B-29 that dropped the Hiroshima bomb was named after the pilot's mother.**
**Case: The Trinity Test. The first atomic bomb test on July 16, 1945. Oppenheimer watched in awe and horror.**
**Case: The Surrender on the Missouri. Japan surrendered aboard the USS Missouri in Tokyo Bay on September 2, 1945.**
**Case: The MacArthur Occupation. MacArthur oversaw Japan's transformation from enemy to ally.**
**Case: The Philippine Campaign. MacArthur returned to the Philippines in 1944, fulfilling his promise.**
**Case: The Fat Man. The Nagasaki bomb was called Fat Man. It was a plutonium implosion bomb.**
**Case: Little Boy. The Hiroshima bomb was called Little Boy. It was a uranium gun-type bomb.**
